messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
The ants first meet at \(t=\frac{15}{2}\) s.  
After this collision ant A heads left and falls off at  

\[
t_A = \frac{15}{2}+\frac{65}{6}= \frac{55}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

At the same instant (\(t=\frac{55}{3}\) s) ants B and C meet at position \(\frac{130}{3}\) cm, then reverse directions.  

* Ant B now moves left a distance \(\frac{130}{3}\) cm at 1 cm/s, so it falls at  

\[
t_B = \frac{55}{3} + \frac{130}{3}= \frac{185}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

* Ant C moves right a distance \(100-\frac{130}{3}= \frac{170}{3}\) cm at 2 cm/s, so it falls at  

\[
t_C = \frac{55}{3} + \frac{85}{3}= \frac{140}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

The last ant to leave the rod is ant B, at time \(\displaystyle \frac{185}{3}\) seconds.

**Answer**

ANSWER: 185/3
